author: Sam Altman
content: Reasoning models are going to get dramatically better over the next year. The jump from where we are now to reliable multi-step planning is closer than most people think.

author: Gary Marcus
content: Another day, another benchmark that quietly drops as soon as you change the surface form of the question. These systems still don't reason; they pattern-match. Show me robustness, not cherry-picked demos.

author: Nathan Lambert
content: A detailed look at how RLHF is being replaced by more scalable preference-learning pipelines, and why the data flywheel matters more than the algorithm. Includes notes on reward model overfitting.

author: Some Account
content: Just landed in SF for the week, who wants to grab coffee and talk shop? DMs open!

author: DeepMind
content: We introduce a new approach to mechanistic interpretability that scales sparse autoencoders to frontier models, recovering interpretable features across layers and enabling targeted interventions.

author: Alignment Researcher
content: A careful argument that current evals systematically overstate agentic capability because they leak affordances the model wouldn't have in deployment. Proposes a harder, deployment-faithful protocol.

author: AI Startup
content: 🚀 Big news! Our new AI tool is 10x faster. Sign up now and get 50% off your first month. Link in bio. #AI #productivity #startup

author: Lab Researcher
content: Spent the week on scaling laws for tool-use agents. Early signal: capability scales smoothly with trajectory data, but reliability has a sharp threshold. More to share once we've replicated.
